message:
feat(cli): improve graph command output with skill names and formatted tables

Enhance the graph analysis output to show human-readable skill names
alongside IDs, and present results in formatted tables for better
readability.

Graph command improvements (graph.rs):
- Build name_map from loaded skills for ID-to-name resolution
- Pass name_map to insights, keystones, and bottlenecks commands

Insights subcommand:
- Show actual cycle, keystone, and bottleneck entries (not just counts)
- Call print_cycles_table() to display cycle details
- Call print_metric_table() for keystones and bottlenecks

Keystones/Bottlenecks subcommands:
- Add MetricEntry struct to hold resolved metric data
- Add resolve_metric_items() to parse bv output and resolve names
- Display formatted table with columns: Rank, Score, Skill ID, Name
- Handle both object format {id, value} and array format [id, value]
  from bv output for compatibility

Table formatting:
- Right-aligned rank and score columns
- Fixed-width skill ID column (36 chars)
- Left-aligned name column
- Proper header separators

Test additions (graph/skills.rs):
- Add test_skills_to_issues_labels_and_status test
- Verify deprecated skills map to Closed status
- Verify quality_score maps to priority (high quality = low priority)
- Verify tags are included in issue labels
- Verify layer is added as "layer:<name>" label

Example output:
  Keystones (showing 10):
  Rank      Score Skill ID                             Name
  ----      ----- --------                             ----
     1     0.8542 rust-error-handling                  Rust Error Handling
     2     0.7891 async-patterns                       Async Programming Patterns

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.5 <noreply@anthropic.com>
